questions.question: Let X be a metric space and let A ⊆ X be a subset. Suppose that every open ball of radius 1/2 centered at a point of A is contained in A. Prove that A is arc-wise connected. Mark [20].
Consider a metric space X and a subset A ⊆ X. If every open ball of radius 1/3 centered at a point of A contains at least one point of A, prove that A is locally connected. Mark [15].

Let (X,d) be a metric space and let A ⊆ X be a non-empty subset. Suppose A is arc-wise connected, but not locally connected. Then, show that there exist points x,y ∈ A such that the distance d(x,y) > 1 and d(x,z) ≤ 1 for all z ∈ A. Mark [20].
